results.summary: Put the feed's side buttons on Liquid Glass, tightened and lowered the stack, and moved Settings into the Profile tab
results.detailed_description: The 'side buttons' are the vertical action rail on the right edge of the feed (Answer this question, Browse questions, Show/Hide question, and Settings). I made all four changes you asked for. (1) Liquid glass: every button now sits on Apple's Liquid Glass instead of the old translucent-material circle — the primary Answer button gets tinted glass, the rest plain regular glass, and they're wrapped in a GlassEffectContainer so the column blends into one cluster. On anything before iOS 26 it falls back to the old material chip so it still reads over the video. (2) Moved down: dropped the rail's bottom inset from 132 to 108pt. (3) Tighter spacing: reduced the gap between buttons from 20 to 12pt. Together with removing the gear (below) the rail takes noticeably less screen. (4) Settings moved into the account tab: the gear is gone from the feed rail and now lives in the Profile tab's navigation bar (top-right gear), opening the same Settings sheet next to the rest of the account controls. I verified the Liquid Glass API usage against the installed iOS 26.5 SDK but did not build (the flow builds/deploys downstream), so the exact new spacing and vertical position are unverified on-device — the inset and spacing values are easy to nudge if they need it.
results.identified_issue: Interpreted 'side buttons' as the feedActionRail (the right-edge TikTok-style column: Answer/Browse/Show-question/Settings). 'Liquid glass' = Apple's iOS 26 Liquid Glass, so used .glassEffect + GlassEffectContainer gated behind #available(iOS 26.0, *) with the previous .ultraThinMaterial circle kept as the pre-26 fallback (deployment target is iOS 17). Verified every symbol (glassEffect, GlassEffectContainer, Glass.regular/.tint/.interactive, .circle shorthand) against the iOS 26.5 SDK swiftinterface. Did not build per the flow's rules. The exact bottom inset (132->108) and spacing (20->12) are visual judgement calls; if the stack now crowds the question banner or the move is too subtle/too much, those two numbers are the knobs.

description: The side button should all have a liquid glass background and also move them down slightly and just decrease the spacing between them so they take up less screen real estate. Finally the settings button could be moved into the account tab.
